The cluster centers are calculated by averaging the coordinates of the points within each cluster. For C1, the points are (0,6) and (6,0), resulting in a center of ((0+6)/2, (6+0)/2) which equals (3,3). For C2, the points are (2,2), (4,4), and (6,6), yielding a center of ((2+4+6)/3, (2+4+6)/3) which equals (4,4). For C3, the points are (5,5) and (7,7), producing a center of ((5+7)/2, (5+7)/2) which equals (6,6). Comparing these calculated values to the options provided, Option A matches the derived centers of (3,3), (4,4), and (6,6).
******
To determine the correct cluster centers, we compute the arithmetic mean of the x and y coordinates for every point assigned to each cluster. The first cluster C1 contains (0,6) and (6,0), so the mean x is 3 and the mean y is 3, giving a centroid of (3,3). The second cluster C2 holds (2,2), (4,4), and (6,6), so the mean x is 4 and the mean y is 4, giving a centroid of (4,4). The third cluster C3 includes (5,5) and (7,7), so the mean x is 6 and the mean y is 6, giving a centroid of (6,6). These results confirm that the set of centers (3,3), (4,4), and (6,6) corresponds exactly to Option A.
